level_signals: Expected to define long-term mission and charter for a newly formed team
Operates at both strategic and highly technical levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong experience as a Platform Product Manager, ideally in data infrastructure or distributed systems environments.
Deep understanding of data architecture and large-scale systems.
Experience working with event-driven systems, high-volume ingestion pipelines, or complex data movement patterns.
Comfortable engaging in highly technical discussions around pipelines, infrastructure, and system evolution.
Proven ability to define structure and clarity in ambiguous, newly forming teams.
Skilled at stakeholder management across multiple technical organizations.
Able to think strategically while managing highly technical execution details.
This is a hybrid role that requires 3 days/week in our Boston office. Fully remote candidates will not be considered at this time.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong experience as a Platform Product Manager, ideally in data infrastructure or distributed systems environments.

scope.surface: Data Ingestion Platform — newly formed team responsible for data ingestion into Klaviyo's core data objects
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform health / reduction of legacy technical debt
core_responsibilities: Define the long-term mission, charter, and ownership boundaries for a newly formed team.
Develop and maintain a coherent, high-impact roadmap that balances foundational investments with active initiatives.
Refine prioritization and sequencing across multiple concurrent projects to ensure focus on the highest-value work.
Own the strategy and roadmap for ingesting large volumes of data from numerous sources.
Think holistically about data movement, transformation, and reliability at scale.
Partner with engineering to design ingestion systems that are scalable, resilient, and maintainable.
Navigate tradeoffs across performance, latency, cost, and reliability.
Drive improvements in observability, monitoring, and operational excellence for ingestion systems.
Identify and drive architectural simplifications that improve platform health over time.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clearly scoped, high-impact platform role at a critical architectural junction
Newly formed team means significant influence over charter, roadmap, and culture
JD explicitly encourages close-but-not-exact-match candidates to apply
Clear articulation of why the role matters and what success looks like
red_flags: Fully remote candidates explicitly excluded; must be in Boston 3 days/week
No years-of-experience floor stated, making seniority bar somewhat ambiguous
Visa sponsorship not mentioned at all
Newly formed team may come with organizational ambiguity and undefined processes

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2B SaaS ecommerce platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD statement 'salary range reflects the cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets')
ambiguities: The 'ideally in data infrastructure or distributed systems environments' qualifier on the Platform PM experience requirement is phrased as a preference within an otherwise required item; extracted as a separate nice-to-have to capture both readings.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD; both set to 'unclear'.
